{
  "market_assessment": {
    "bias": "BULLISH",
    "reasoning": "Broad-based rally with sector leadership across IT (COFORGE +11%), capital goods (TITAGARH +9.8%, GRSE +9.2%), FMCG (MARICO +4.8%), financials (ABCAPITAL +6.6%, CAMS +10.5%), and chemicals (NAVINFLUOR +3.7%). Most held positions are showing solid in-the-money moves. Global cues strong (S&P +1.46%, Nasdaq +2.02%). Risk: book is fully deployed across 11 names, several near or at 52-week highs — focus must be on trailing SLs to lock gains, not adding more.",

      "nifty_support": 24000,
      "nifty_resistance": 24500
    }
  },

      "timeframe": "SWING",
      "reasoning": "CMP 1056.70, +7.4% from entry 984. Current SL 1035 is already above entry (locked-in gain ~5%). CMP-to-SL gap is ~2.1% — comfortable. Q4 strong (PAT +19%, GNPA <1%), bullish trend intact. SL stays at 1035 (no change needed — already at trail level satisfying the 1% gap rule). HOLD effectively."

Trail SL from 1720 to 1725 — tightens by 0.3%, less than 0.5% threshold so this is below the micro-adjust floor. Holding SL at 1720, no change needed. Convert to HOLD."

    },
    {

      "exchange": "NSE",

    },

    },

      "product": "CNC",

      "timeframe": "SWING",

  "position_actions": [

  "watchlist_notes": "Strong tape but book is already 11 names ~80%+ deployed; no fresh entries today. HFCL pending order from yesterday (1828min) — at 11:15 the ticker is irrelevant to my current book. CANCEL not requested via this schema; will let it expire/fill at the limit if intraday order. BAJAJ-AUTO and BHARATFORG today are interesting but: BAJAJ-AUTO post-buyback price already +1.3% near 52w high (chase risk after 7%+ pre-result run); BHARATFORG reports today (binary). Both stay on watch but no entry. HDFCBANK setup unconfirmed (chairman vacancy overhang). LUPIN, AUROPHARMA — pharma earnings binary. NIFTYBEES — book already proxy-long the index via 11 holdings; no point doubling.",
  "portfolio_notes": "Cumulative P&L is reported as -53.72% but realized losses to date show -₹3,173 in the rolling-5-day section, suggesting the headline figure may reflect mark-to-market on positions valued at CMP=0 in this snapshot (data feed shows zero CMPs in holdings table — clearly a quote-feed glitch). Treating actual book risk via the per-stock CMP figures shown in the Deep Dive section, which are non-zero and consistent. Win-rate 14% over 5 days is concerning — discipline focus today is: (1) no new entries on top of an already-full book, (2) trail SLs as winners advance, (3) prep HAL trim/exit ahead of May 14 binary. Defense/capital-goods cluster (HAL + GRSE + TITAGARH + BHARATFORG-watched) at ~3 names is at the cluster ceiling — no further additions. Cash buffer effectively zero deployable; the soft 80% rule is the active ceiling — wait for an EXIT before any new BUY."
}
